facts: Ann Baker began breeding in Riverside, California.
Foundation cat Josephine, a white Persian/Angora-type, owned by neighbor Penny Robinson.
Founding cats: Daddy Warbucks, Fugianna, and Buckwheat.
Baker trademarked the name Ragdoll in 1971.
Founded the IRCA (International Ragdoll Cat Association) in 1971.
Required strict contracts dictating breeding, naming, and sales.
Denny and Laura Dayton broke from Baker's IRCA.
Founded RFCI to push for mainstream recognition.
Breed crossed the Atlantic in 1981 via UK imports.
Ragdoll debuted at the CFA International Show in Nashville, 1993.
CFA accepted Ragdoll for registration during the decade.
Bicolor Ragdolls advanced to CFA Provisional status in 1998.
CFA granted full Championship status in 2000.
TICA had already granted championship recognition.
Popularity surged worldwide as a premier show breed.
Ranked CFA's #1 most popular breed multiple consecutive years.
Became the most popular cat breed in the world per CFA stats.
Dominates social media and modern pet culture.

today.summary: The Ragdoll is currently the world's most popular cat breed per CFA registrations, prized for its docile, dog-like temperament and the signature limp 'ragdoll' flop when held.
facts: One of the largest domestic breeds; males 15-20 lbs.
Semi-longhaired with captivating blue eyes and pointed coat.
Goes limp when picked up, hence the name 'Ragdoll'.
Recognized by CFA, TICA, GCCF, and most major registries.
Known for getting along with kids, dogs, and other pets.
